detected_facts: Source filename: synthetic-broken-references.json.
Fixture ID: synthetic-reference-001.
Export format version: 2.
Account ID: 9001; container ID: 5102; public ID: GTM-SYN502.
Entity counts observed: 1 tag, 1 trigger, 1 variable, 1 folder, 0 custom template, 1 built-in variable, 0 zone.
findings.recommendation: Use this inventory as audit coverage context. No GTM configuration change is proposed from this finding.

detected_facts: Tag 102 named "GA4 - Event - Purchase" has firingTriggerId "299".
No trigger with triggerId "299" exists in the exported trigger array.
The export does contain trigger 202 named "CE - purchase" but this is not the referenced ID.
findings.inferred_issue: The GA4 purchase event tag may not fire as intended because its firing trigger reference is unresolved in the export.
findings.recommendation: Manual review required. Confirm the intended firing trigger in GTM or source documentation. A likely candidate by name is trigger 202, "CE - purchase", but the MLP does not automatically repair ambiguous trigger references.

detected_facts: Tag 102 named "GA4 - Event - Purchase" has parentFolderId "499".
No folder with folderId "499" exists in the exported folder array.
The export does contain folder 402 named "GA4 - Ecommerce" but this is not the referenced ID.
findings.inferred_issue: The tag's folder assignment is unresolved. This is primarily an organization and reviewability issue, not direct firing behavior.
findings.recommendation: Manual review required. Confirm intended folder assignment. A likely candidate by name is folder 402, "GA4 - Ecommerce", but the MLP does not automatically repair ambiguous folder references.

detected_facts: Tag 102 named "GA4 - Event - Purchase" parameter key "value" references variable token "{{Missing Revenue}}".
No variable named "Missing Revenue" exists in the exported variable array.
The export contains variable 302 named "DLV - ecommerce.transaction_id" with dataLayer key "ecommerce.transaction_id"; this does not match the referenced token.
findings.inferred_issue: The GA4 purchase event value may resolve to an undefined value, which may affect ecommerce revenue reporting.
findings.recommendation: Manual review required. Confirm the intended revenue variable and dataLayer key before adding, renaming, or remapping any variable. Do not change ecommerce value mapping automatically from the export alone.

detected_facts: Tag 102 is a GA4 event-style tag with eventName "purchase".
The tag includes a value parameter set to "{{Missing Revenue}}".
The referenced revenue variable is unresolved in the export.
The export contains a transaction ID dataLayer variable, but no observed currency or items variable references on the purchase tag.
findings.inferred_issue: Purchase revenue reporting may be incomplete or broken because the value parameter references a missing variable. Currency and item array mappings were not detected from the export.
findings.recommendation: Review ecommerce dataLayer documentation and validate a test purchase in GTM Preview. Confirm revenue, currency, transaction_id, and items mappings before making any payload change.

detected_facts: Trigger 202 is present but no tag references triggerId 202.
Folder 402 is present but no exported entity uses parentFolderId 402.
Variable 302 is present but no exported parameter references {{DLV - ecommerce.transaction_id}}.
findings.inferred_issue: These entities appear orphaned from the exported relationships. They may be intended targets for the broken references, but that cannot be confirmed from ID/name mismatch alone.
findings.recommendation: Do not delete or archive automatically. Review whether these are the intended trigger, folder, and transaction ID variable for the purchase tag.

manual_review_items.item: Confirm the intended firing trigger for tag 102 before changing firingTriggerId 299.
manual_review_items.reason: Changing trigger references can alter purchase tracking behavior.

manual_review_items.item: Confirm intended folder assignment for tag 102 before changing parentFolderId 499.
manual_review_items.reason: Folder candidate 402 is plausible but not deterministic from the export alone.

manual_review_items.item: Confirm intended revenue variable and dataLayer key for purchase value before adding, renaming, or remapping variables.
manual_review_items.reason: The unresolved {{Missing Revenue}} token may affect ecommerce revenue reporting.

manual_review_items.item: Review apparently orphaned trigger 202, folder 402, and variable 302 before archive, delete, or reuse decisions.
manual_review_items.reason: They may be intended targets for broken references, but the export does not prove that.

assumptions: The uploaded fixture JSON is the only GTM container export input for this run.
No client-specific override was provided beyond the active user instruction.
A tag type of gaawe with eventName purchase is treated as a GA4 event-style ecommerce purchase tag for audit purposes.
Likely target references are noted only as candidates, not as confirmed intent.
limitations: This is file-in/file-out analysis of an exported GTM configuration only.
No live GTM, GA4, Google Ads, website, API, workspace, or production platform was inspected or modified.
Validation does not prove live tracking correctness, consent behavior, legal compliance, privacy compliance, or publish safety.
